1.Once Data Coll ection is complete, follow the prompts to finalize and unload the collection
plate from the instrument. Depending on your sequencing platform and configuration, you
will either Download the readout package
 or Send the run to BaseSpace. For details, refer
to the GeoMx DSP Instrument User Manual (MAN-10152), NGS Readout User Manual 
(MAN-10153), and (if applicable) Spatial Proteogenomic 
Assay User Manual (MAN-10158).
2.Use a dry-down seal and your laboratory’s procedure to dry aspirates
(e.g., dry at 65°C in thermal cycler with open lid).
3.Depending on the analyte, add volume of nuclease-free water (see table), pipette up and
down 5x, and let stand at room temperature (RT) for 10 minutes. Quick-spin.

Quick Reference Guide
GeoMx DSP —NGS Readout1.Prepare a dilution in a new tube by combining 2 μ L of libr ary and 14
μL of Elution Buffer.
2.Asses
s quality of library stock and dilution, and purified NTC, using
a capillary electrophoresis device such as Agilent Bioanalyzer® or
TapeStation®.
3.Check for the e
xpected size of the amplicons and absence of primers,
primer-dimers, or high molecular weight overamplification products.Expected library amplicon size is 162-166 bp. With the Bioanalyzer,the library will appear as ~150 bp; with the TapeStation, ~170 bp.
4.
Asses
s quantity of library using Qubit® or qPCR.Quality control4
1.Prepare library for sequencing by spiking in 1-5% PhiXand bringing t
o a final loading concentration per Illumina
platform and flow cell specifications. Some examples arelisted in the table.
2.
Prepar
e Illumina SampleSheet.csv by copying and pasting
contents of the Indices.csv from the GeoMx readoutpackage (does not apply to NextSeq 1000/2000 users).
3.
Sequence libr
ary on Illumina sequencing platform with the
following specifications:
• Index reads: 2 indexes
• Read type: Paired-end• Read length: read 1 = 27 bp; read 2 = 27 bp• Index 1 (i7) and Index 2 (i5): 8 bp each for Seq Code
assays, 10 bp each for Pro Code assays. See GeoMxDSP NGS Readout User Manual (MAN-10153) for details.

27 bp; read 2 = 27 bp• Index 1 (i7) and Index 2 (i5): 8 bp each for Seq Code
assays, 10 bp each for Pro Code assays. See GeoMxDSP NGS Readout User Manual (MAN-10153) for details.
• Generate FASTQ (if available on your platform)
4.

t steps in NGS Readout depend on your

Refer to the Dilute and Denature Guide provided by Illumina for your 
specific sequencing platform. Depending on the platform and workflow, the Loading Concentration listed above may describe the solution that is pipetted into the cartridge or the final concentration of what is sequenced after onboard dilution.

Pipeline Method*
Standard Standalone Pipeline
1. Set up sequencing run
a. On-instrument
b. On BaseSpace (for NextSeq
1000/2000 only)
2. Sequence
3. Convert BCL to FASTQ ﬁles
a. On BaseSpace
b. On-instrumentc. Oﬀ-Instrument
4. If needed, transfer FASTQ ﬁles
to a location for Pipelineaccessibility 
5. Process FASTQ ﬁles in 
standalone GeoMx NGS Pipelinea. Installed locally (GUI)b. Installed on server 
(Linux, AWS)
6. Upload DCC.zip to GeoMx
See instructions for Option 1 in the 
GeoMx DSP NGS Readout User Manual (MAN-10153).DRAGEN Pipeline on BaseSpace

and conﬁg.ini ﬁle to BaseSpace

BaseSpace
